levels.description: For students who need additional scaffolding, visual supports, and hands-on guidance to engage with living things concepts. These students benefit from reduced complexity, repeated exposure, and teacher proximity during activities.
strategies: Use picture-based vocabulary cards with real photographs instead of illustrations
Provide graphic organizers with pre-filled sections to reduce writing demands
Offer tactile learning experiences with real specimens (leaves, rocks, plastic animals)
Implement peer tutoring pairing with confident learners
Use verbal and physical prompts during sorting activities
Simplify instructions to 1-2 step directions
Provide sentence stems for verbal responses (e.g., 'A living thing can ___')
Offer color-coded sorting bins (green=living, gray=non-living)
Allow use of manipulatives instead of written responses
Provide whispered audio guides during nature walk components

activities.name: Touch & Sort
activities.description: Students physically sort real objects (feather, leaf, rock, plastic toy) into living/non-living bins with teacher guidance
activities.scaffolding: Teacher holds student's hand during sorting, provides verbal cues

activities.name: Picture Walk
activities.description: Teacher-led virtual walk through local environment using large photographs, students point to identify living things
activities.scaffolding: Reduced set of 4-5 clear images, immediate positive reinforcement

activities.name: Needs Match
activities.description: Students match pre-labeled pictures of needs (water, sun, food) to a single plant or animal with physical cards
activities.scaffolding: Only 3 items to match, visual-only responses allowed

levels.description: For students working at grade-level expectations. These students engage with the full curriculum through standard activities, independent practice, and collaborative learning with appropriate challenge.
strategies: Present content through multiple modalities (visual, auditory, kinesthetic)
Use think-pair-share for processing questions
Provide open-ended exploration time during investigations
Offer choice in how students demonstrate learning (drawing, labeling, verbal)
Include both structured and unstructured activity time
Use grade-level vocabulary with explicit teaching of key terms
Facilitate small group discussions with diverse learners
Provide immediate feedback during practice activities
Connect concepts to students' own experiences and environments

activities.name: Living Things Scavenger Hunt
activities.description: Students explore the classroom or outdoor area to find and record 8 living things on their checklist
activities.differentiation: Core version includes 8 items, some visual cues provided

activities.name: Sort & Explain
activities.description: Students sort picture cards into living/non-living categories and explain their reasoning to a partner
activities.differentiation: Students write or verbalize 1 reason for each choice

activities.name: Plant Needs Diagram
activities.description: Students label a plant diagram with water, sunlight, and soil, then predict what happens if one is missing
activities.differentiation: Includes word bank and example prediction sentence

activities.name: Animal Movement Match
activities.description: Students match animals to their movement types (walk, fly, swim, crawl) and create a movement freeze frame
activities.differentiation: 16 animals to match, includes extension question

levels.description: For students who demonstrate advanced understanding and need enrichment. These students engage with deeper thinking, real-world applications, and independent research opportunities.
strategies: Offer open-ended inquiry questions without predetermined answers
Provide research opportunities using age-appropriate media
Encourage students to create their own questions for investigation
Include leadership roles in group activities (teacher's helper, team captain)
Challenge with 'what if' scenarios and prediction tasks
Connect to higher-grade concepts in simplified ways
Allow extended time for deeper exploration of interests
Offer choice in complex project-based learning
Invite students to help peers as 'expert' teachers
Introduce basic scientific vocabulary and classification

activities.name: Living Things Detective
activities.description: Students investigate a mystery scenario where they must determine if something is living based on multiple criteria, defending their answer with evidence
activities.challenge: Requires justification using all 4 characteristics of living things

activities.name: Design a Habitat
activities.description: Students design a habitat for a chosen animal, explaining how it meets all the animal's needs
activities.challenge: Includes consideration of food chain, shelter, and climate

activities.name: Needs Experiment
activities.description: Students predict and observe what happens to a plant when one need is removed over multiple days, recording findings
activities.challenge: Independent investigation with hypothesis, data collection, and conclusion

activities.name: Animal Adaptation Explorer
activities.description: Students research how different animals have adapted to survive, comparing 3+ animals and presenting findings
activities.challenge: Compares adaptations across environments, connects to survival needs
